001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassArrayFormat.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassNameFormat.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
025
026import java.lang.annotation.*;
027import java.lang.reflect.*;
028import java.util.*;
029import java.util.function.*;
030import java.util.stream.*;
031
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
036
037/**
038 * Lightweight utility class for introspecting information about a method or constructor parameter.
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * This class provides a convenient wrapper around {@link Parameter} that extends the standard Java reflection
042 * API with additional functionality for parameter introspection, annotation handling, and name resolution.
043 * It supports resolving parameter names from bytecode (when compiled with <c>-parameters</c>) or from
044 * <ja>@Name</ja> annotations.
045 *
046 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
047 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
048 *    <li>Parameter introspection - access parameter metadata, type, annotations
049 *    <li>Name resolution - resolve parameter names from bytecode or annotations
050 *    <li>Qualifier support - extract qualifier names from annotations
051 *    <li>Hierarchy traversal - find matching parameters in parent methods/constructors
052 *    <li>Annotation support - get annotations declared on the parameter
053 * </ul>
054 *
055 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
056 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
057 *    <li>Introspecting parameter metadata for code generation or analysis
058 *    <li>Resolving parameter names for dependency injection frameworks
059 *    <li>Finding annotations on parameters
060 *    <li>Working with parameter types and qualifiers
061 *    <li>Building frameworks that need to analyze method/constructor signatures
062 * </ul>
063 *
064 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
065 * <p class='bjava'>
066 *    <jc>// Get ParameterInfo from a method</jc>
067 *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ...;
068 *    ParameterInfo <jv>param</jv> = <jv>mi</jv>.getParameters().get(0);
069 *
070    *    <jc>// Get parameter type</jc>
071    *    ClassInfo <jv>type</jv> = <jv>param</jv>.getParameterType();
072    *
073    *    <jc>// Get resolved name (from bytecode or @Name annotation)</jc>
074    *    String <jv>name</jv> = <jv>param</jv>.getResolvedName();
075    *
076    *    <jc>// Get annotations</jc>
077    *    List&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt;&gt; <jv>annotations</jv> =
078    *       <jv>param</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).toList();
079    * </p>
080    *
081    * <h5 class='section'>Parameter Name Resolution:</h5>
082    * <p>
083    * Parameter names are resolved in the following order:
084    * <ol class='spaced-list'>
085    *    <li><ja>@Name</ja> annotation value (if present)
086    *    <li>Bytecode parameter names (if compiled with <c>-parameters</c> flag)
087    *    <li><c>arg0</c>, <c>arg1</c>, etc. (fallback if names unavailable)
088    * </ol>
089    *
090    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
091    *    <li class='jc'>{@link MethodInfo} - Method introspection
092    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ConstructorInfo} - Constructor introspection
093    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ExecutableInfo} - Common executable functionality
094    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsReflection">Reflection Package</a>
095    * </ul>
096    */
097   @SuppressWarnings({
098      "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
099   })
100public final class ParameterInfo extends ElementInfo implements Annotatable {
101
102   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
103   private static final String ARG_inner = "inner";
104
105   /**
106    * Resettable supplier for the system property to disable bytecode parameter name detection.
107    *
108    * <p>
109    * When the value is <jk>true</jk>, parameter names will only come from <ja>@Name</ja>
110    * annotations and not from bytecode parameter names (even if compiled with <c>-parameters</c> flag).
111    *
112    * <p>
113    * This can be set via system property: <c>juneau.disableParamNameDetection=true</c>
114    *
115    * <p>
116    * The supplier can be reset for testing purposes using {@link #resetDisableParamNameDetection()}.
117    */
118   static final Memoizer<Boolean> DISABLE_PARAM_NAME_DETECTION = memoizer(() -> Settings.get().get("juneau.disableParamNameDetection").asBoolean().orElse(false));
119
120   /**
121    * Creates a ParameterInfo wrapper for the specified parameter.
122    *
123    * <p>
124    * This convenience method automatically determines the declaring executable from the parameter
125    * and finds the matching ParameterInfo.
126    *
127    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
128    * <p class='bjava'>
129    *    Parameter <jv>p</jv> = ...;
130    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ParameterInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>p</jv>);
131    * </p>
132    *
133    * @param inner The parameter being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
134    * @return A ParameterInfo object wrapping the parameter.
135    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the parameter is <jk>null</jk> or cannot be found in its declaring executable.
136    */
137   public static ParameterInfo of(Parameter inner) {
138      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
139      var exec = inner.getDeclaringExecutable();
140      ExecutableInfo execInfo;
141      if (exec instanceof Constructor<?> c)
142         execInfo = ConstructorInfo.of(c);
143      else
144         execInfo = MethodInfo.of((Method)exec);
145      return execInfo.getParameters().stream()
146         .filter(x -> eq(x.inner(), inner))
147         .findFirst()
148         .orElse(null);
149   }
150
151   static void reset() {
152      DISABLE_PARAM_NAME_DETECTION.reset();
153   }
154
155   private final ExecutableInfo executable;
156   private final Parameter inner;
157
158   private final int index;
159   private final ClassInfo type;
160   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>>> annotations;  // All annotations declared directly on this parameter.
161   private final Supplier<List<ParameterInfo>> matchingParameters;  // Matching parameters in parent methods.
162
163   private final Memoizer<String> resolvedName = memoizer(this::findNameInternal);  // Resolved name from @Name annotation or bytecode.
164
165   private final Memoizer<String> resolvedQualifier = memoizer(this::findQualifierInternal);  // Resolved qualifier from @Named or @Qualifier annotation.
166
167   private final Supplier<String> toString;  // String representation with modifiers, type, name, and varargs flag.
168
169   /**
170    * Constructor.
171    *
172    * <p>
173    * Creates a new ParameterInfo wrapper for the specified parameter. This constructor is protected
174    * and should not be called directly. ParameterInfo instances are typically obtained from
175    * {@link ExecutableInfo#getParameters()} or {@link #of(Parameter)}.
176    *
177    * @param executable The ExecutableInfo (MethodInfo or ConstructorInfo) that contains this parameter.
178    * @param inner The parameter being wrapped.
179    * @param index The zero-based index of this parameter in the method/constructor signature.
180    * @param type The ClassInfo representing the parameter type.
181    */
182   protected ParameterInfo(ExecutableInfo executable, Parameter inner, int index, ClassInfo type) {
183      super(inner.getModifiers());
184      this.executable = executable;
185      this.inner = inner;
186      this.index = index;
187      this.type = type;
188      this.annotations = memoize(() -> stream(inner.getAnnotations()).flatMap(AnnotationUtils::streamRepeated).map(a -> ai(this, a)).toList());
189      this.matchingParameters = memoize(this::findMatchingParameters);
190      this.toString = memoize(this::findToString);
191   }
192
193   /**
194    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parameter can accept the specified value.
195    *
196    * @param value The value to check.
197    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parameter can accept the specified value.
198    */
199   public boolean canAccept(Object value) {
200      return getParameterType().canAcceptArg(value);
201   }
202
203   @Override /* Annotatable */
204   public AnnotatableType getAnnotatableType() { return AnnotatableType.PARAMETER_TYPE; }
205
206   /**
207    * Returns an {@link AnnotatedType} object that represents the use of a type to specify the type of this parameter.
208    *
209    * <p>
210    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#getAnnotatedType()}.
211    *
212    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
213    * <p class='bjava'>
214    *    <jc>// Get annotated type: void method(@NotNull String value)</jc>
215    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
216    *    AnnotatedType <jv>aType</jv> = <jv>pi</jv>.getAnnotatedType();
217    *    <jc>// Check for @NotNull on the type</jc>
218    * </p>
219    *
220    * @return An {@link AnnotatedType} object representing the type of this parameter.
221    * @see Parameter#getAnnotatedType()
222    */
223   public AnnotatedType getAnnotatedType() { return inner.getAnnotatedType(); }
224
225   /**
226    * Returns all annotations declared on this parameter.
227    *
228    * <p>
229    * Returns annotations directly declared on this parameter, wrapped as {@link AnnotationInfo} objects.
230    *
231    * <p>
232    * <b>Note on Repeatable Annotations:</b>
233    * Repeatable annotations (those marked with {@link Repeatable @Repeatable}) are automatically
234    * expanded into their individual annotation instances. For example, if a parameter has multiple {@code @Marshalled} annotations,
235    * this method returns each {@code @Marshalled} annotation separately, rather than the container annotation.
236    *
237    * @return
238    *    An unmodifiable list of annotations on this parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
239    *    <br>Repeatable annotations are expanded into individual instances.
240    */
241   public List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>> getAnnotations() { return annotations.get(); }
242
243   /**
244    * Returns a stream of annotation infos of the specified type declared on this parameter.
245    *
246    * @param <A> The annotation type.
247    * @param type The annotation type.
248    * @return A stream of annotation infos, never <jk>null</jk>.
249    */
250   @SuppressWarnings({
251      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for annotation stream
252   })
253   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> type) {
254      return getAnnotations().stream().filter(x -> x.isType(type)).map(x -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)x);
255   }
256
257   /**
258    * Returns the constructor that this parameter belongs to.
259    *
260    * @return The constructor that this parameter belongs to, or <jk>null</jk> if it belongs to a method.
261    */
262   public ConstructorInfo getConstructor() { return executable.isConstructor() ? (ConstructorInfo)executable : null; }
263
264   /**
265    * Returns the {@link ExecutableInfo} which declares this parameter.
266    *
267    * <p>
268    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#getDeclaringExecutable()} but returns {@link ExecutableInfo} instead.
269    *
270    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
271    * <p class='bjava'>
272    *    <jc>// Get the method or constructor that declares this parameter</jc>
273    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
274    *    ExecutableInfo <jv>executable</jv> = <jv>pi</jv>.getDeclaringExecutable();
275    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>executable</jv>.isConstructor()) {
276    *       ConstructorInfo <jv>ci</jv> = (ConstructorInfo)<jv>executable</jv>;
277    *    }
278    * </p>
279    *
280    * @return The {@link ExecutableInfo} declaring this parameter.
281    * @see Parameter#getDeclaringExecutable()
282    */
283   public ExecutableInfo getDeclaringExecutable() { return executable; }
284
285   /**
286    * Returns the index position of this parameter.
287    *
288    * @return The index position of this parameter.
289    */
290   public int getIndex() { return index; }
291
292   @Override /* Annotatable */
293   public String getLabel() {
294      var exec = getDeclaringExecutable();
295      var label = exec.getDeclaringClass().getNameSimple() + "." + exec.getNameShort();
296      return label + "[" + index + "]";
297   }
298
299   /**
300    * Returns this parameter and all matching parameters in parent classes.
301    *
302    * <p>
303    * For constructors, searches parent class constructors for parameters with matching name and type,
304    * regardless of parameter count or position. This allows finding annotated parameters that may be
305    * inherited by child classes even when constructor signatures differ.
306    *
307    * <p>
308    * For methods, searches matching methods (same signature) in parent classes/interfaces
309    * for parameters at the same index with matching name and type.
310    *
311    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
312    * <p class='bjava'>
313    *    <jc>// Constructor with different parameter counts:</jc>
314    *    <jk>class</jk> A {
315    *       A(String <jv>foo</jv>, <jk>int</jk> <jv>bar</jv>) {}
316    *    }
317    *    <jk>class</jk> B <jk>extends</jk> A {
318    *       B(String <jv>foo</jv>) {}
319    *    }
320    *    <jc>// For B's foo parameter, returns: [B.foo, A.foo]</jc>
321    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
322    *    List&lt;ParameterInfo&gt; <jv>matching</jv> = <jv>pi</jv>.getMatchingParameters();
323    * </p>
324    *
325    * @return A list of matching parameters including this one, in child-to-parent order.
326    */
327   public List<ParameterInfo> getMatchingParameters() { return matchingParameters.get(); }
328
329   /**
330    * Returns the method that this parameter belongs to.
331    *
332    * @return The method that this parameter belongs to, or <jk>null</jk> if it belongs to a constructor.
333    */
334   public MethodInfo getMethod() { return executable.isConstructor() ? null : (MethodInfo)executable; }
335
336   /**
337    * Returns the Java language modifiers for the parameter represented by this object, as an integer.
338    *
339    * <p>
340    * The {@link Modifier} class should be used to decode the modifiers.
341    *
342    * <p>
343    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#getModifiers()}.
344    *
345    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
346    * <p class='bjava'>
347    *    <jc>// Check if parameter is final</jc>
348    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
349    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>modifiers</jv> = <jv>pi</jv>.getModifiers();
350    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>isFinal</jv> = Modifier.<jsm>isFinal</jsm>(<jv>modifiers</jv>);
351    * </p>
352    *
353    * @return The Java language modifiers for this parameter.
354    * @see Parameter#getModifiers()
355    * @see Modifier
356    */
357   @Override
358   public int getModifiers() { return inner.getModifiers(); }
359
360   /**
361    * Returns the name of the parameter.
362    *
363    * <p>
364    * Searches for the name in the following order:
365    * <ol>
366    *    <li>@Name annotation value (takes precedence over bytecode parameter names)
367    *    <li>Bytecode parameter name (if compiled with -parameters flag)
368    *    <li>Matching parameters in parent classes/interfaces (for methods)
369    *    <li>Synthetic name like "arg0", "arg1", etc. (fallback)
370    * </ol>
371    *
372    * <p>
373    * This method works with any annotation named "Name" (from any package) that has a <c>String value()</c> method.
374    *
375    * @return The name of the parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
376    * @see Parameter#getName()
377    */
378   public String getName() {
379      var name = getResolvedName();
380      return name != null ? name : inner.getName();
381   }
382
383   /**
384    * Returns a {@link Type} object that identifies the parameterized type for this parameter.
385    *
386    * <p>
387    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#getParameterizedType()}.
388    *
389    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
390    * <p class='bjava'>
391    *    <jc>// Get generic type information for parameter: List&lt;String&gt; values</jc>
392    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
393    *    Type <jv>type</jv> = <jv>pi</jv>.getParameterizedType();
394    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>type</jv> <jk>instanceof</jk> ParameterizedType) {
395    *       ParameterizedType <jv>pType</jv> = (ParameterizedType)<jv>type</jv>;
396    *       <jc>// pType.getActualTypeArguments()[0] is String.class</jc>
397    *    }
398    * </p>
399    *
400    * @return A {@link Type} object identifying the parameterized type.
401    * @see Parameter#getParameterizedType()
402    */
403   public Type getParameterizedType() { return inner.getParameterizedType(); }
404
405   /**
406    * Returns the class type of this parameter.
407    *
408    * @return The class type of this parameter.
409    */
410   public ClassInfo getParameterType() { return type; }
411
412   /**
413    * Finds the name of this parameter for bean property mapping.
414    *
415    * <p>
416    * Searches for the parameter name in the following order:
417    * <ol>
418    *    <li><ja>@Name</ja> annotation value
419    *    <li>Bytecode parameter name (if available and not disabled via system property)
420    *    <li>Matching parameters in parent classes/interfaces
421    * </ol>
422    *
423    * <p>
424    * This method is used for mapping constructor parameters to bean properties.
425    *
426    * <p>
427    * <b>Note:</b> This is different from {@link #getResolvedQualifier()} which looks for <ja>@Named</ja>
428    * annotations for bean injection purposes.
429    *
430    * @return The parameter name if found, or <jk>null</jk> if not available.
431    * @see #getResolvedQualifier()
432    * @see #getName()
433    */
434   public String getResolvedName() { return resolvedName.get(); }
435
436   /**
437    * Finds the bean injection qualifier for this parameter.
438    *
439    * <p>
440    * Searches for the <ja>@Named</ja> or <ja>@Qualifier</ja>
441    * annotation value to determine which named bean should be injected.
442    *
443    * <p>
444    * This method is used by the {@link BeanStore} for bean injection.
445    *
446    * <p>
447    * <b>Note:</b> This is different from {@link #getResolvedName()} which looks for <ja>@Name</ja>
448    * annotations for bean property mapping.
449    *
450    * @return The bean qualifier name if {@code @Named} or {@code @Qualifier} annotation is found, or <jk>null</jk> if not annotated.
451    * @see #getResolvedName()
452    */
453   public String getResolvedQualifier() { return resolvedQualifier.get(); }
454
455   /**
456    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the parameter has a name.
457    *
458    * <p>
459    * This returns <jk>true</jk> if the parameter has an annotation with the simple name "Name",
460    * or if the parameter's name is present in the class file.
461    *
462    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the parameter has a name.
463    */
464   public boolean hasName() {
465      return getResolvedName() != null;
466   }
467
468   /**
469    * Returns the wrapped {@link Parameter} object.
470    *
471    * @return The wrapped {@link Parameter} object.
472    */
473   public Parameter inner() {
474      return inner;
475   }
476
477   /**
478    * Compares this ParameterInfo with the specified object for equality.
479    *
480    * <p>
481    * Two ParameterInfo objects are considered equal if they wrap the same underlying {@link Parameter} object.
482    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Parameter#equals(Object)} method.
483    *
484    * <p>
485    * This method makes ParameterInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
486    * and {@link HashSet}.
487    *
488    * @param obj The object to compare with.
489    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
490    */
491   @Override
492   public boolean equals(Object obj) {
493      return obj instanceof ParameterInfo other && eq(this, other, (x, y) -> eq(x.inner, y.inner));
494   }
495
496   /**
497    * Returns a hash code value for this ParameterInfo.
498    *
499    * <p>
500    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Parameter#hashCode()} method.
501    *
502    * <p>
503    * This method makes ParameterInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
504    * and {@link HashSet}.
505    *
506    * @return A hash code value for this ParameterInfo.
507    */
508   @Override
509   public int hashCode() {
510      return inner.hashCode();
511   }
512
513   @Override
514   public boolean is(ElementFlag flag) {
515      return switch (flag) {
516         case SYNTHETIC -> isSynthetic();
517         case NOT_SYNTHETIC -> ! isSynthetic();  // HTT
518         case VARARGS -> isVarArgs();
519         case NOT_VARARGS -> ! isVarArgs();
520         default -> super.is(flag);
521      };
522   }
523
524   /**
525    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parameter is implicitly declared in source code.
526    *
527    * <p>
528    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parameter is neither explicitly nor implicitly declared in source code.
529    *
530    * <p>
531    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#isImplicit()}.
532    *
533    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
534    * <p class='bjava'>
535    *    <jc>// Filter out implicit parameters</jc>
536    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
537    *    <jk>if</jk> (! <jv>pi</jv>.isImplicit()) {
538    *       <jc>// Process explicit parameter</jc>
539    *    }
540    * </p>
541    *
542    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parameter is implicitly declared.
543    * @see Parameter#isImplicit()
544    */
545   public boolean isImplicit() { return inner.isImplicit(); }
546
547   /**
548    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the parameter has a name according to the <c>.class</c> file.
549    *
550    * <p>
551    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#isNamePresent()}.
552    *
553    * <p>
554    * <b>Note:</b> This method is different from {@link #hasName()} which also checks for the presence
555    * of a <c>@Name</c> annotation. This method only checks if the name is present in the bytecode.
556    *
557    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
558    * <p class='bjava'>
559    *    <jc>// Check if parameter name is in bytecode</jc>
560    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
561    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>pi</jv>.isNamePresent()) {
562    *       String <jv>name</jv> = <jv>pi</jv>.getName();
563    *    }
564    * </p>
565    *
566    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the parameter has a name in the bytecode.
567    * @see Parameter#isNamePresent()
568    * @see #hasName()
569    */
570   public boolean isNamePresent() { return inner.isNamePresent(); }
571
572   /**
573    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parameter is a synthetic construct as defined by the Java Language Specification.
574    *
575    * <p>
576    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#isSynthetic()}.
577    *
578    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
579    * <p class='bjava'>
580    *    <jc>// Filter out compiler-generated parameters</jc>
581    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
582    *    <jk>if</jk> (! <jv>pi</jv>.isSynthetic()) {
583    *       <jc>// Process real parameter</jc>
584    *    }
585    * </p>
586    *
587    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parameter is a synthetic construct.
588    * @see Parameter#isSynthetic()
589    */
590   public boolean isSynthetic() { return inner.isSynthetic(); }
591
592   /**
593    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the parameter type is an exact match for the specified class.
594    *
595    * @param c The type to check.
596    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the parameter type is an exact match for the specified class.
597    */
598   public boolean isType(Class<?> c) {
599      return getParameterType().is(c);
600   }
601   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
602   // High Priority Methods (direct Parameter API compatibility)
603   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
604
605   /**
606    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parameter represents a variable argument list.
607    *
608    * <p>
609    * Same as calling {@link Parameter#isVarArgs()}.
610    *
611    * <p>
612    * Only returns <jk>true</jk> for the last parameter of a variable arity method.
613    *
614    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
615    * <p class='bjava'>
616    *    <jc>// Check if this is a varargs parameter</jc>
617    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
618    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>pi</jv>.isVarArgs()) {
619    *       <jc>// Handle variable arguments</jc>
620    *    }
621    * </p>
622    *
623    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parameter represents a variable argument list.
624    * @see Parameter#isVarArgs()
625    */
626   public boolean isVarArgs() { return inner.isVarArgs(); }
627
628   /**
629    * Returns a detailed string representation of this parameter.
630    *
631    * <p>
632    * The returned string includes:
633    * <ul>
634    *    <li>Modifiers (final)
635    *    <li>Parameter type with generics (e.g., "List&lt;String&gt;")
636    *    <li>Parameter name (if available)
637    *    <li>Varargs indicator (if applicable)
638    * </ul>
639    *
640    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
641    * <p class='bjava'>
642    *    <jc>// Simple parameter</jc>
643    *    ParameterInfo <jv>pi</jv> = ...;
644    *    <jv>pi</jv>.toString();
645    *    <jc>// Returns: "java.lang.String name"</jc>
646    *
647    *    <jc>// Final parameter</jc>
648    *    <jc>// Returns: "final java.lang.String name"</jc>
649    *
650    *    <jc>// Generic parameter</jc>
651    *    <jc>// Returns: "java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt; items"</jc>
652    *
653    *    <jc>// Varargs parameter</jc>
654    *    <jc>// Returns: "java.lang.String... values"</jc>
655    *
656    *    <jc>// Parameter without name (fallback)</jc>
657    *    <jc>// Returns: "int arg0"</jc>
658    * </p>
659    *
660    * @return A detailed string representation including modifiers, type, name, and varargs flag.
661    */
662   @Override
663   public String toString() {
664      return toString.get();
665   }
666
667   private String findToString() {
668      var sb = new StringBuilder(128);
669
670      // Modifiers (final is common for parameters)
671      var mods = Modifier.toString(getModifiers());
672      if (nn(mods) && ! mods.isEmpty()) {
673         sb.append(mods).append(" ");
674      }
675
676      // Parameter type (use generic type if available to show generics)
677      var paramType = getParameterizedType();
678
679      // For varargs, we need to get the component type and display it with "..." instead of "[]"
680      if (isVarArgs()) {
681         // Get the component type of the array
682         var typeInfo = ClassInfo.of(paramType);
683         var componentType = typeInfo.getComponentType();
684         // Display the component type without array brackets, then add "..."
685         componentType.appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
686         sb.append("...");
687      } else {
688         ClassInfo.of(paramType).appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
689      }
690
691      // Parameter name (if available)
692      var name = getResolvedName();
693      if (nn(name)) {
694         sb.append(" ").append(name);
695      } else {
696         // Fallback to index-based name if no name available
697         sb.append(" arg").append(index);
698      }
699
700      return sb.toString();
701   }
702
703   private List<ParameterInfo> findMatchingParameters() {
704      if (executable instanceof ConstructorInfo executable2) {
705         // For constructors: search parent class constructors for parameters with matching index and type
706         // Note: We match by index and type only, not by name, to avoid circular dependency
707         // (getName() needs getMatchingParameters() which needs getName())
708         var list = new ArrayList<ParameterInfo>();
709
710         // Add this parameter first
711         list.add(this);
712
713         // Search parent classes for matching parameters
714         var cc = executable2.getDeclaringClass().getSuperclass();
715         while (nn(cc)) {
716            // Check all constructors in parent class
717            for (var pc : cc.getDeclaredConstructors()) {
718               // Check if constructor has parameter at this index with matching type
719               var params = pc.getParameters();
720               if (index < params.size() && getParameterType().is(params.get(index).getParameterType())) {
721                  list.add(params.get(index));
722               }
723            }
724            cc = cc.getSuperclass();
725         }
726
727         return list;
728      }
729      // For methods: use matching methods from parent classes
730      return ((MethodInfo)executable).getMatchingMethods().stream().map(m -> m.getParameter(index)).toList();
731   }
732
733   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
734   // Annotatable interface methods
735   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
736
737   private String findNameInternal() {
738      // Search through matching parameters in hierarchy for @Name annotations only.
739      // Note: We intentionally prioritize @Name annotations over bytecode parameter names
740      // because bytecode names are unreliable - users may or may not compile with -parameters flag.
741      for (var mp : getMatchingParameters()) {
742         for (var ai : mp.getAnnotations()) {
743            if (ai.hasNameSimple("Name")) {
744               var value = ai.getValue().orElse(null);
745               if (value != null)  // HTT
746                  return value;
747            }
748         }
749      }
750
751      return o(inner).filter(x -> x.isNamePresent()).filter(x -> ! DISABLE_PARAM_NAME_DETECTION.get()).map(x -> x.getName()).orElse(null);
752   }
753
754   private String findQualifierInternal() {
755      // Search through matching parameters in hierarchy for @Named or qualifier-meta annotations.
756      // @formatter:off
757      return getMatchingParameters().stream()
758         .flatMap(mp -> mp.getAnnotations().stream())
759         .map(JsrSupport::qualifierValue)
760         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
761         .findFirst()
762         .orElse(null);
763      // @formatter:on
764   }
765
766   /**
767    * Checks if this parameter can be resolved from the bean store.
768    *
769    * <p>
770    * The following parameter types are considered optional and always return <jk>true</jk>:
771    * <ul>
772    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>Optional&lt;T&gt;</c>
773    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>T[]</c>, <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c>, <c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c>, or <c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c>
774    * </ul>
775    *
776    * <p>
777    * If a parameter has a <ja>@Named</ja>or <ja>@Qualifier</ja> annotation,
778    * the method checks for a bean with that specific name in the bean store.  Otherwise, it checks for an unnamed bean
779    * of the parameter type in the bean store, and if not found, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
780    *
781    * @param beanStore The bean store to check for beans.
782    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to check if not found in the bean store.
783    *    These are checked after the bean store but before returning <jk>false</jk>.
784    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parameter can be resolved, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
785    */
786   public boolean canResolve(BeanStore beanStore, Object... otherBeans) {
787      var pt = getParameterType();
788
789      // @Value sites resolve from Settings/Env, not the bean store — always considered resolvable
790      // here. (Mutual-exclusion with @Inject is enforced in resolveValue(...).)
791      if (ValueResolver.hasValueAnnotation(getAnnotations()))
792         return true;
793
794      if (pt.is(Optional.class) || pt.isInjectCollectionType()) // Optional/Collection/array types are always satisfied (even if empty).
795         return true;
796
797      var bq = getResolvedQualifier();
798
799      if (nn(bq))
800         return beanStore.hasBean(pt.inner(), bq);
801
802      if (beanStore.hasBean(pt.inner()))
803         return true;
804
805      for (var o : otherBeans)
806         if (canAccept(o))
807            return true;
808      return false;
809   }
810
811   /**
812    * Returns the missing type name if this parameter cannot be resolved, <jk>null</jk> otherwise.
813    *
814    * <p>
815    * The following parameter types are considered optional and always return <jk>null</jk>:
816    * <ul>
817    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>Optional&lt;T&gt;</c>
818    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>T[]</c>, <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c>, <c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c>, or <c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c>
819    * </ul>
820    *
821    * <p>
822    * If a parameter has a <ja>@Named</ja> or <ja>@Qualifer</ja> annotation,
823    * the method checks for a bean with that specific name in the bean store.  Otherwise, it checks for an unnamed bean
824    * of the parameter type in the bean store, and if not found, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
825    *
826    * @param beanStore The bean store to check for beans.
827    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to check if not found in the bean store.
828    *    These are checked after the bean store but before marking the parameter as missing.
829    * @return Missing type name (e.g., <js>"String"</js> or <js>"String@name"</js> for qualified beans) or <jk>null</jk> if resolvable.
830    */
831   public String getMissingType(BeanStore beanStore, Object... otherBeans) {
832      var pt = getParameterType();
833      if (ValueResolver.hasValueAnnotation(getAnnotations()))
834         return null;
835      if (pt.is(Optional.class) || pt.isInjectCollectionType()) // Optional/Collection/array types are always satisfied (even if empty).
836         return null;
837      var bq = getResolvedQualifier();  // Use @Named/@Qualified for bean injection
838      if (nn(bq)) {
839         if (! beanStore.hasBean(pt.inner(), bq))
840            return pt.getNameSimple() + '@' + bq;
841         return null;
842      }
843      if (beanStore.hasBean(pt.inner()))
844         return null;
845      for (var o : otherBeans)
846         if (canAccept(o))
847            return null;
848      return pt.getNameSimple();
849   }
850
851   /**
852    * Resolves the parameter value from the bean store.
853    *
854    * <p>
855    * For each parameter, this method:
856    * <ul>
857    *    <li>If the parameter is a collection/array/map type, collects all beans of the element type.
858    *    <li>Otherwise, looks up a single bean by type and optional qualifier name in the bean store.
859    *    <li>If not found in the bean store, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
860    * </ul>
861    *
862    * <h5 class='section'>Parameter Resolution:</h5>
863    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
864    *    <li><b>Single beans</b> - Resolved using {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class)} or {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class, String)}.
865    *       If not found, checks <c>otherBeans</c> for a compatible instance.
866    *       Throws {@link ExecutableException} if not found in either location.
867    *    <li><b>Optional beans</b> - Wrapped in <c>Optional</c>, or <c>Optional.empty()</c> if not found.
868    *       Never throws an exception.
869    *    <li><b>Arrays</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into an array (may be empty).
870    *       Never throws an exception.
871    *    <li><b>Lists</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>List</c> (may be empty).
872    *       Never throws an exception.
873    *    <li><b>Sets</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashSet</c> (may be empty).
874    *       Never throws an exception.
875    *    <li><b>Maps</b> - All beans of the value type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashMap</c> keyed by bean name (may be empty).
876    *       Unnamed beans use an empty string as the key.  Never throws an exception.
877    * </ul>
878    *
879    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve beans from.
880    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to use if not found in the bean store.
881    *    These are checked after the bean store but before throwing an exception.
882    * @return The resolved parameter value.
883    * @throws ExecutableException If a required parameter (non-Optional, non-collection) cannot be resolved
884    *    from the bean store or <c>otherBeans</c>.
885    */
886   
887   @SuppressWarnings({
888      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
889      "java:S6541", // Single-threaded context; synchronization unnecessary
890      "unchecked"   // reflective Provider<T> resolution — valueType is Class<?> at runtime; cast narrows it to Class<Object> for the supplier signature
891   })
892   public Object resolveValue(BeanStore beanStore, Object... otherBeans) {
893      var pt = getParameterType();
894      var bq = getResolvedQualifier();
895      var ptu = pt.unwrap(Optional.class);
896
897      // @Value sites resolve from Settings/VarResolver, not the bean store.
898      var annos = getAnnotations();
899      var valueExpr = ValueResolver.findValueExpression(annos);
900      if (valueExpr != null) {
901         ValueResolver.checkInjectConflict(annos, this.toString());
902         // Pass the declared (generic) parameter type so @Value Supplier<String> autodetects
903         // Supplier<String> field/parameter type opts in to re-evaluating reads.
904         // BeanStore is forwarded so caller-scoped PropertySource beans (e.g. per-RestContext
905         // @Rest(config=...) Configs) participate in expression resolution alongside Settings.
906         var resolved = ValueResolver.resolve(valueExpr, ptu.inner(), inner.getParameterizedType(), this.toString(), beanStore);
907         // Mirror FieldInfo.inject: wrap in Optional when the declared parameter type is Optional<T>.
908         // VarResolver substitutes "" for a missing key with no default — collapse both null and ""
909         // to Optional.empty() so @Value("${maybe}") Optional<T> behaves the same as Spring's.
910         if (pt.is(Optional.class))
911            return (resolved == null || (resolved instanceof String s && s.isEmpty()))
912               ? oe()
913               : o(resolved);
914         return resolved;
915      }
916
917      if (JsrSupport.isProviderType(ptu.inner())) {
918         var valueType = ptu.innerType() instanceof ParameterizedType parameterizedType
919            && parameterizedType.getActualTypeArguments().length == 1
920            && parameterizedType.getActualTypeArguments()[0] instanceof Class<?> c
921               ? c
922               : Object.class;
923         var supplier = (Supplier<Object>)() -> beanStore.getBean((Class<Object>)valueType, bq).orElse(null);
924         var providerType = ptu.inner();
925         if (eq(providerType.getName(), JsrSupport.JUNEAU_PROVIDER))
926            return (Provider<Object>)supplier::get;
927         var handler = (InvocationHandler)(proxy, method, args) -> {
928            if (eq(method.getName(), "get") && method.getParameterCount() == 0)
929               return supplier.get();
930            if (eq(method.getName(), "toString") && method.getParameterCount() == 0)
931               return providerType.getSimpleName() + "(" + valueType.getSimpleName() + ")";
932            if (eq(method.getName(), "hashCode") && method.getParameterCount() == 0)
933               return System.identityHashCode(proxy);
934            if (eq(method.getName(), "equals") && method.getParameterCount() == 1)
935               return proxy == args[0];
936            throw exex("Unsupported provider method: {0}", method.getName());
937         };
938         return Proxy.newProxyInstance(providerType.getClassLoader(), new Class<?>[] {providerType}, handler);
939      }
940
941      // Handle collections and arrays
942      Object collectionValue = null;
943      if (ptu.isInjectCollectionType()) {
944         // Extract element type from collection/array/map parameter type
945         Class<?> elementType = null;
946
947         // Handle arrays
948         if (ptu.isArray()) {
949            elementType = ptu.getComponentType().inner();
950         } else {
951            // Get the parameterized type
952            Type parameterizedType = getParameterizedType();
953            if (parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
954               var rawType = pt2.getRawType();
955               // If wrapped in Optional, unwrap it to get the nested type
956               if (rawType instanceof Class<?> rawClass && rawClass == Optional.class) {
957                  var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
958                  if (typeArgs.length > 0 && typeArgs[0] instanceof ParameterizedType typeArgs2) {
959                     // Optional<List<T>> -> List<T>
960                     parameterizedType = typeArgs2;
961                  } else {
962                     // Optional<SomeClass> - not a collection, elementType remains null
963                     parameterizedType = null;
964                  }
965               }
966            } else if (pt.innerType() instanceof ParameterizedType) {
967               // If pt is already a parameterized type (e.g., List<TestService> after unwrapping Optional),
968               // use pt.innerType() as the parameterizedType
969               parameterizedType = pt.innerType();
970            } else {
971               parameterizedType = null;
972            }
973
974            // Handle List<T> or Set<T>
975            var inner2 = o(ptu.inner()).orElse(Object.class);
976            if (eq(inner2, List.class) || eq(inner2, Set.class)) {
977               if (parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
978                  var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
979                  if (typeArgs.length > 0 && typeArgs[0] instanceof Class<?> elementClass) {
980                     elementType = elementClass;
981                  }
982               }
983            } else if (eq(inner2, Map.class) && parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
984               // Handle Map<String,T> - extract value type (second type argument)
985               var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
986               // Verify key type is String and get value type
987               if (typeArgs.length >= 2 && typeArgs[0] == String.class && typeArgs[1] instanceof Class<?> valueClass) {
988                  elementType = valueClass;
989               }
990            }
991         }
992
993         collectionValue = ReflectionUtils.resolveCollectionValue(elementType, beanStore, ptu);
994      }
995      if (nn(collectionValue))
996         return pt.is(Optional.class) ? o(collectionValue) : collectionValue;
997
998      // Handle single bean
999      var ptc = ptu.inner();
1000
1001      Optional<Object> r;
1002
1003      if (nn(bq)) {
1004         r = beanStore.getBean(ptc, bq);
1005      } else {
1006         r = beanStore.getBean(ptc);
1007         if (r.isEmpty()) {
1008            for (var r2 : otherBeans)
1009               if (canAccept(r2)) {
1010                  r = o(r2);
1011                  break;
1012               }
1013         }
1014      }
1015
1016      if (pt.is(Optional.class))
1017         return r;
1018      if (r.isPresent())
1019         return r.get();
1020
1021      throw exex("Could not resolve value for parameter {0}", this);
1022   }
1023
1024   /**
1025    * Resolves the parameter value from the bean store (constructor version with enclosingInstance).
1026    *
1027    * <p>
1028    * This method is used for constructor parameters.  If the first parameter type matches the <c>enclosingInstance</c>
1029    * object type, it uses the <c>enclosingInstance</c> object (for non-static inner class constructors).
1030    * Otherwise, it delegates to {@link #resolveValue(BeanStore, Object...)}.
1031    *
1032    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve beans from.
1033    * @param enclosingInstance The outer class instance for non-static inner class constructors.
1034    *    If the first parameter type matches this object's type, it is used as the first parameter value.
1035    *    Can be <jk>null</jk> for regular classes or static inner classes.
1036    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to use if not found in the bean store.
1037    *    These are checked after the bean store but before throwing an exception.
1038    * @return The resolved parameter value.
1039    * @throws ExecutableException If a required parameter (non-Optional, non-collection) cannot be resolved
1040    *    from the bean store or <c>otherBeans</c>.
1041    */
1042   public Object resolveValue(BeanStore beanStore, Object enclosingInstance, Object... otherBeans) {
1043      var pt = getParameterType();
1044      if (getIndex() == 0 && nn(enclosingInstance) && pt.isInstance(enclosingInstance))
1045         return enclosingInstance;
1046      return resolveValue(beanStore, otherBeans);
1047   }
1048}
